strengths: ~17 years of continuous software engineering across embedded, web, mobile, and infrastructure domains — meets the minimum tenure bar
Entrepreneurial experience as founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, ongoing since 2016) demonstrates builder mindset and product ownership
Active knowledge-sharing through public talks (FOSS4G 2019) and multi-day workshops shows capacity to inspire and educate technical communities
concerns: AI expertise is superficial and very recent — limited to MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling since April 2025, with no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale
No evidence of director-level IC scope: no strategic customer advisory, no cross-org technical vision setting, no cloud or AI platform design at enterprise scale
Education does not meet preferred qualifications — two bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts), no Master's or PhD, and no AI-adjacent academic depth
response.justification: The role demands recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products or platforms, strategic advisory capability at a director IC level, and ideally an advanced AI-related degree. The candidate's AI involvement is confined to a single role started in April 2025, contributing MCP tooling — too narrow and too recent to evidence the 'recognized expertise' the role requires. There is no record of advising strategic customers, shaping cloud or AI platform strategy, or operating at director-equivalent scope in any position. The candidate's broad engineering background (embedded systems, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby, cloud-native work at Optilyz) and GISCollective founder experience are genuine assets, but they do not close the gap on the core AI strategy and leadership competencies this role is built around.
